title: Verbally Viscous Moroccan Pork Shoulder Tangia
description: Funky, chewy Moroccan dish featuring pork shoulder, papillote, served in a claypot.
instructions: Score the pork shoulder in a crisscross pattern to help it cook evenly.
In a small bowl, mix together cumin, cayenne pepper, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Rub the spice mixture all over the pork shoulder, making sure to get some under the skin as well.
Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the pork shoulder on all sides until it's nicely browned, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Place the prunes on the bottom of a claypot, followed by the browned pork shoulder.
Add the chopped onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger on top of the pork shoulder.
Drizzle the lemon juice over the onion mixture.
Cover the claypot with foil and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 hours, or until the pork is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
Remove the foil and continue baking for another 30 minutes to crisp up the skin.
Let the pork rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing it thinly and serving.
